items.hook: Saleem Sinai is the protagonist of the Booker Prize-winning novel Midnight's Children by Salman Rushdie.
items.body: His life is closely intertwined with the events that take place in his homeland of pre- and post-colonial India, and newly created Pakistan and Bangladesh. Worth a quiet trip down the rabbit hole.

items.hook: World War II: Claus von Stauffenberg and four fellow conspirators are executed for the July 20 plot to assassinate Adolf Hitler.
items.body: Claus Philipp Maria Justinian Schenk Graf von Stauffenberg was a German army officer who is best known for his failed attempt on 20 July 1944 to assassinate Adolf Hitler at the Wolf's Lair, part of Operation Valkyrie. The world is stranger than it lets on.

items.hook: BBC Introducing stage axed from Reading Festival
items.body: The stage has hosted up-and-coming artists including Ed Sheeran at the Berkshire festival since 2008.

items.hook: The agency became one of Peru's most innovative and awarded agencies, pioneering digital marketing in the country and achieving significant international recognition before its integration into VML in 2024.
items.body: Phantasia was a Peruvian marketing and advertising agency founded in 1998 by José-Carlos Mariátegui and Paul Thorndike. One of those things you can't un-know.

items.hook: A Bangladesh Air Force Chengdu FT-7BGI crashes shortly after takeoff into Milestone School campus in Dhaka, Bangladesh, killing 35 people and injuring 173.
items.body: The Bangladesh Air Force is the aerial warfare branch of the Bangladesh Armed Forces, responsible for defending the airspace of Bangladesh and supporting national security operations. Bring it up somewhere. Watch the reaction.

items.hook: The journal publishes articles on oncology, pediatric oncology, hemato-oncology, and oncopathology.
items.body: The Indian Journal of Medical and Paediatric Oncology is a peer-reviewed open-access medical journal published by Medknow Publications on behalf of the Indian Society of Medical and Paediatric Oncology. Bring it up somewhere. Watch the reaction.

items.hook: How to unlock unlimited geothermal energy, anywhere we want
items.body: Earth contains more than enough heat to power humanity many times over, but so far only a few lucky places have been able to exploit it. Now we may have a way to tap this vast clean energy source almost anywhere

items.hook: Brunello: The Gracious Visionary review – fawning portrait of the king of cashmere
items.body:
Giuseppe Tornatore traces Italian fashion designer Brunello Cucinelli’s life from poor farming family to billion-dollar empire in a sentimental vanity project

Brunello Cucinelli is the Italian fashion designer and entrepreneur who

items.hook: The 100 best novels of all time
items.body: The top 100 novels of all time published in English, as voted for by authors, critics and academics worldwide. How many have you read?

items.hook: ‘Witty, engaging and absurd’: LensCulture critics’ choice award winners – in pictures
items.body: Is that a rooftop crocodilian? Can you cause a forest fire in a matchbox? Things are not always as they seem with the winners of this year’s Lensculture critics’ choice awards

items.opener: Here's one for the group chat —
items.hook: GraceLand is a 2004 novel by Chris Abani, which tells the story of a teenager named Elvis, who is trying to get out of the ghettos of Lagos, Nigeria.
items.body: Chris Abani depicts the poverty and violence in Lagos and how it affects the everyday lives of Elvis and his family. Bring it up somewhere. Watch the reaction.

items.hook: Mercury program: Mercury-Redstone 4 mission: Gus Grissom piloting Liberty Bell 7 becomes the second American to go into space (in a suborbital mission).
items.body: Project Mercury was the first human spaceflight program of the United States, running from 1958 through 1963. Bring it up somewhere. Watch the reaction.

items.opener: Here's one for the group chat —
items.hook: e is a comic novel by Matt Beaumont first published in 2000.
items.body: Written in the epistolary tradition, it consists entirely of e-mails written between the employees of an advertising agency and some of their business partners. Bring it up somewhere. Watch the reaction.

items.hook: The world's lowest temperature in an inhabited location is recorded at Vostok Station, Antarctica at −89.2 °C (−128.6 °F).
items.body: The lowest natural temperature ever directly recorded at ground level on Earth is −89.2 °C at the then-Soviet Vostok Station in Antarctica on 21 July 1983 by ground measurements. Bring it up somewhere. Watch the reaction.

items.hook: Christopher Ewart-Biggs, the British ambassador to the Republic of Ireland, is assassinated by the Provisional IRA.
items.body: Christopher Thomas Ewart Ewart-Biggs was the British ambassador to Ireland, an author, and a senior Foreign Office liaison officer with MI6. Bring it up somewhere. Watch the reaction.

items.hook: The Trail of the Lonesome Pine is a 1908 romance novel/western novel by John Fox, Jr.
items.body: The novel became Fox's most successful, and was included among the top ten list of bestselling novels for 1908 and 1909. One of those things you can't un-know.

items.hook: Georgian organisations claim state festivals are being hijacked by “authoritarian regime”
items.body: Top Georgian groups say international guests are being used as “useful idiots”.

items.hook: Splatoon Raiders review – Nintendo’s inky shooter fades to grey
items.body:
Nintendo Switch 2
The single-player offshoot of the quirky and colourful multiplayer series is disappointingly run-of-the-mill

Splatoon is among Nintendo’s most ingenious inventions of the last decade: a series

items.hook: The Mysterious Benedict Society is a novel written by Trenton Lee Stewart and illustrated by Carson Ellis, first published in 2007.
items.body: It tells the story of four gifted children: Reynie Muldoon, George "Sticky" Washington, Kate Wetherall, and Constance Contraire, who together form the "Mysterious Benedict Society" and are sent to investigate an institution called L.I.V.E., run by a man named

items.hook: At the conclusion of a fireworks display on Okura Beach in Akashi, Hyōgo, Japan, 11 people are killed and more than 120 are injured when a pedestrian footbridge connecting the beach to JR Asagiri Station becomes overcrowded and people leaving the event fall down in a domino effect.
items.body: Akashi is a city in southern Hyōgo Prefecture, Japan. One of those things you can't un-know.
